Product Design & Research RentSpree · 2025
Jump to Context Format The Work Outcomes

2025 AI Design Jam & PXD Summit

A three-day design jam where RentSpree's PXD team came together to imagine, prototype, and pitch the future of AI in rental housing, turning abstract possibilities into tangible concepts and igniting a shared excitement about where the product could go next.

AI/ML Strategy Product Design Hackathon Team Engagement Innovation Future Visioning Concept Design Agentic UX
6 Designers & PMs contributing
8 AI concepts explored
1 Summit day of ideation
2 Award-winning projects
∞ Ideas sparked
The Context

Why We Did This

AI was accelerating everywhere, but a roadmap briefing wasn't enough. The PXD team needed to feel it, build it, and own a piece of the future themselves. HackSpree created the space to turn abstract potential into something real, visible, and worth getting excited about.

⚡
Rapid Industry Shift
AI was reshaping renter and landlord expectations across PropTech. The team needed to internalize what that meant for RentSpree customers: not just read about it, but experience building for it.
🧭
Shared Vision, Built Together
As the company explored an agentic product direction, PXD needed a shared vocabulary around what "AI-native" means for renters, landlords, and agents. Making together was the fastest path to alignment.
🔥
Ownership and Motivation
Designers do their best work when they're genuinely excited about the problem. HackSpree gave every contributor a personal stake in imagining what RentSpree could become, and the energy to push it there.
"The goal wasn't to ship these concepts; it was to build the muscle of imagining, so that every person on the team could feel that the future of RentSpree runs through their work." PXD Summit, 2025
The Format

How We Structured the Event

The summit was designed to move fast, from inspiration to concept to pitch across session. Each contributor chose a problem they cared about and explored it through an AI lens, culminating in team presentations and a community vote.

1
Framing the Future State
The event opened with a shared prompt: how does AI change what's possible in rental housing? Context was set around agentic OS concepts and the two-sided marketplace opportunity at RentSpree.
2
Choose Your Problem Space
Each participant self-selected a domain: lease management, lead conversion, landlord tools, renter experience, or platform intelligence, and began exploring AI-native solutions.
3
Design Jam: Build & Prototype
With time-boxed sprints, contributors sketched, wireframed, and built out their concepts, leaning into visual storytelling and interaction models that showed AI in action.
4
Present & Spark Discussion
Each person presented their concept to the group, fielding questions and building on each other's thinking. The goal: spark ideas, not just pitch a finished product.
5
Community Vote & Awards
The team voted on their favorites: for both technical ambition and for the concept most likely to shape how RentSpree thinks about AI. Winners received recognition across the company.
6
Carry the Ideas Forward
The best concepts seeded real product conversations, informed roadmap discussions, and gave the team a shared reference point for what "agentic RentSpree" could look and feel like.
The Work

Six Visions of an Agentic Future

Each contributor explored a different moment in the rental journey where AI could eliminate friction, surface the right action, or give users back time. Together they sketch a picture of what RentSpree becomes when intelligence is woven into every touchpoint.

Platform Strategy
Agentic OS

Today, renters, landlords, and agents each navigate a different version of RentSpree, with disconnected contexts, redundant effort, missed signals. Agentic OS reimagines the platform as a living layer of intelligence that routes the right information to the right person at the right moment, without anyone having to ask. Renters stop hunting for status updates. Landlords stop managing inboxes. Agents stay focused on relationships instead of logistics. The platform becomes the coordinator that everyone wishes they had.

Platform Architecture Two-Sided Marketplace Agentic Design
🥉 3rd Place: BuildSpree
Ellis: The RentSpree Concierge

Lease endings are one of the most stressful, ambiguous moments for renters. Ellis removes that anxiety entirely. Instead of chasing down landlords, guessing at timelines, and managing decisions in a dozen different places, renters get a single guided experience: Ellis surfaces their options, explains the tradeoffs, and moves everything forward: renewal, relocation, or transition, with minimal friction. Landlords benefit too, arriving at a decision with a renter who already understands their choices and feels supported throughout.

Renter Experience Lease Renewal AI Concierge Proactive Guidance
Concept Entry
Smart Threads

For landlords, a lease renewal shouldn't require managing three email threads, a portal login, and a spreadsheet. For renters, it shouldn't feel like a maze of forms and waiting. Smart Threads collapses the whole process into a single conversational space: the AI drafts, both parties respond, and decisions are translated directly into updated lease terms. No follow-up emails. No version confusion. Just a clean, shared thread that moves toward a signed outcome with every message.

Landlord Tools Messaging UX Lease Workflow Collaboration
Concept Entry
Dashboards That Participate

Property managers and landlords currently log into dashboards to learn what already happened. By then, the optimal moment to act has often passed. This concept flips the model: the dashboard monitors conditions continuously and surfaces prepared actions: a refinancing opportunity before rates shift, a renewal offer ready to send before a tenant starts shopping, a vendor task queued before a small repair becomes a costly one. Owners stop reacting and start leading, with the AI doing the watching so they don't have to.

Property Management Proactive UX Landlord Intelligence
Concept Entry
Agent Enablement & Confidence

When an application is declined, everyone loses, and no one feels good about it. Agents struggle to explain a numerical score to a frustrated applicant. Landlords second-guess their own criteria. This concept gives agents the language and the tools to handle those moments with clarity and empathy. The Confidence Scorecard translates a complex risk profile into a plain-language narrative. The What-If Sandbox lets agents explore how different criteria would change an outcome, turning a difficult conversation into a constructive one, and helping landlords set thresholds they'll actually stand behind.

Agent Tools Applicant Experience AI Explainability Screening
🏆 Winner + Popular Vote
Renesis: Concierge AI

Most users never discover the full power of the products they pay for. Renesis closes that gap: not with documentation or tutorials, but with a context-aware copilot that's already inside the product, already knows where the user is, and already understands what they're trying to do. Agents don't need to know what to ask; Renesis anticipates the next step. Landlords get guidance calibrated to their specific properties and tenants. Property managers find the right action surfaced before they've thought to look for it. The product becomes an expert partner, not just a tool.

Embedded Copilot Context-Aware AI Agent Experience Product Intelligence
Concept Entry
AI-Powered Lead Management

The gap between a prospective renter showing interest and submitting an application is where landlords lose the most qualified candidates, usually not because of a bad fit, but because of slow follow-up or a showing that never got scheduled. This AI-native dashboard removes that drop-off entirely: leads are captured with full context summaries, smart follow-up is triggered automatically based on engagement signals, and showings are scheduled without back-and-forth. Landlords fill units faster, renters get timely responses instead of silence, and the best applicants don't walk away to a competitor.

Lead Conversion Landlord Efficiency Renter Responsiveness Automation
The Outcomes

What HackSpree Sparked

The value of HackSpree wasn't a single shipped feature; it was the energy, ownership, and shared vision it created. The team left the summit with something harder to manufacture: genuine excitement about the future they were building together.

6
Team members who experienced AI-native product design firsthand
8
Distinct AI concept spaces explored across the platform
2
Winning concepts recognized for vision and execution
🌱
Concepts That Seeded Strategy
Ideas from HackSpree, especially around agentic product architecture and embedded copilots, directly informed how the team frames AI investment conversations and future roadmap priorities.
🤝
A Shared Language for AI
By building together, designers and PMs developed a concrete shared vocabulary around contextual AI, proactive UX, and what "enhancement not replacement" actually looks like for renters, landlords, and agents.
🚀
A Repeatable Energy Model
HackSpree proved that structured creative autonomy: a focused problem, real time to explore, and a stage to share, is one of the highest-leverage investments a product team can make in itself.
"When a team can point to something they built and say 'that's the future we're working toward': you've done something that no roadmap slide can replicate." HackSpree Retrospective
